articles.title: From US To Israel: Global Leaders Mark One Year Of Pahalgam Terror Attack
articles.description: On the first anniversary of the Pahalgam terror attack, US Ambassador to India Sergio Gor and Israeli Ambassador Reuven Azar paid tribute to the 26 victims and expressed solidarity with India. Gor reaffirmed Washington’s support in the fight against terrorism and extended condolences to the affected families. Azar also remembered the victims in a post on social media, stressing that terrorism has no borders and highlighting Israel’s continued support for India. The remembrance marks a moment of shared grief and global unity against terror following the 2025 attack in Jammu and Kashmir.

articles.title: Beijing denies Trump’s ‘gift’ claim over seized ship bound for Iran
articles.description: China denied US claims that a seized ship carried a “gift” for Iran, rejecting allegations of missile-linked shipments as tensions rise over maritime security in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: ‘Conflict over internal reservation undermining Ambedkar’s vision’

articles.title: India watches, Pakistan acts: The optics war Delhi is losing

Diplomatic access and strategic positioning are key themes.

articles.title: Residents pay the price as Afghanistan-Pakistan border remains closed
articles.description: The border between Pakistan and Afghanistan has been completely closed for six months, a direct consequence of the conflict between Kabul and Islamabad. Despite several mediation attempts, negotiations have failed, and the two countries remain at a diplomatic impasse in a war that has already displaced tens of thousands of people and claimed numerous lives.

articles.title: Pahalgam Attack Anniv: Congress Slams Modi Govt, Calls Pakistan’s Global Standing A ‘Foreign Policy Failure’
articles.description:
The Congress on Wednesday attacked the Modi government on the first anniversary of the Pahalgam terror attack, saying “failed state” Pakistan has now acquired a new global respectability which reflects the “complete failure” of the substance of Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s foreign policy.

In a post on X, Congress general secretary in-charge communications Jairam Ramesh said April 22nd will always be a day of great pain and intense anguish for every Indian.

"Exactly a year ago, the terror attack orchestrated by Pakistan took place in Pahalgam. Our thoughts are with the bereaved families. We also recall the bravery of the young local ponywallah who was shot dead while trying to save a tourist," Ramesh said.

The LG of Jammu and Kashmir admitted it was an intelligence failure, he claimed.

"It is not clear what action was taken on this admission, even though the killers themselves were brought to justice a few months later," Ramesh said.

"Pakistan stood isolated after it had carried out the terror attacks in Mumbai in November 2008," he said.

"Its (Pakistan) economy continues to be in shambles, dependent on largesse from external donors. Separatist movements are deeply entrenched in its polity and society. Its politics is dysfunctional and it is the Army that is calling the shots in the country," Ramesh said.

"Yet today, that very same failed state whose army chief had made inflammatory and communally provocative statements just a few days prior to the deadly deed, has now acquired a new global respectability," he said.

"In many ways, this reflects the complete failure of the substance of the Prime Minister's foreign policy and the self-glorifying style of his diplomatic engagement," Ramesh alleged.

"Should he reset? Of course, yes. Will he reset? Most certainly he will not," the Congress leader said.

The Pakistan-based terror group Lashkar-e-Taiba carried out an attack at Pahalgam in Jammu and Kashmir on this day last year, killing 26 innocent people, mostly tourists from across India.

After the Pahalgam terror attack, India carried out a retaliatory military offensive against Pakistan named Operation Sindoor, in which Indian armed forces destroyed military and terror infrastructure in Pakistan and Pakistan occupied Kashmir.

This report has been published as part of the auto-generated syndicate wire feed. Apart from the headline, no editing has been done in the copy by ABP Live.

articles.title: Ex-Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Nadendla Bhaskara Rao dies at 90
articles.description: Nadendla Bhaskara Rao, who served 31 days as Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister in 1984 after a revolt against N.T. Rama Rao, has died at a Hyderabad hospital at age 90.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu Assembly Elections: 7 key candidates and their constituencies in 2026 polls

The contest is set to be a three-cornered fight between the DMK-led alliance, the AIADMK-anchored NDA, and actor Vijay's fledgling TVK party.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu Assembly Election 2026: Key candidates and constituencies to watch out for
articles.description: Campaigning for the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections has ended, paving the way for single-phase voting across all 234 constituencies on Thursday, with results scheduled for May 4. The election is largely seen as a direct contest between the DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance and the National Democratic Alliance, led in the state by the AIADMK.

articles.title: Trump Extends Ceasefire but Iran Threatens War Over Blockade | Hormuz Crisis Deepens | Latest N18G
articles.description: Tensions escalate sharply as Donald Trump issues a firm warning: there can be no deal if the Strait of Hormuz reopens. The statement signals a hardening stance by Washington even as diplomatic efforts continue in the background.The Strait of Hormuz is one of the world’s most critical energy routes, with a significant share of global oil shipments passing through it. Any decision to maintain or lift a blockade has far-reaching implications—not just for Iran and the U.S., but for global markets and energy security.Trump’s remarks come amid ongoing truce talks, rising military pressure, and increasing uncertainty over whether diplomacy can succeed. Analysts warn that tying a deal to Hormuz control could complicate negotiations further and push both sides toward confrontation. articles.title: 'Won't Stop Fighting': Houthi Chief Says War Not Over Despite Trump Ceasefire Extension
articles.description: The guns are silent, but the blockade remains. President Donald Trump has officially extended the ceasefire with Iran, but there’s a catch. Brokered at the urgent request of Pakistan’s Field Marshal Asim Munir and PM Shehbaz Sharif, this extension is a final window for Tehran to present a unified proposal. With Houthi leaders already promising that "more fighting is coming" and the U.S. Navy maintaining its grip on the Strait of Hormuz, the region is holding its breath. Is this the path to peace or just the calm before the storm?
